verses.text: Miriam and Aaron spake against Moses because of the Ethiopian woman he married: hath the LORD spoken only by Moses? hath He not spoken also by us? The LORD heard. Moses was very meek, above all men on the face of the earth. Guidance: Sibling jealousy disguised as racial criticism, dressed up as a spiritual complaint. God heard every word. The narrator pauses to tell us: Moses is meek — meaning he doesn't fight back. He doesn't have to.

Miriam and Aaron spake against Moses because of the Ethiopian woman he married: hath the LORD spoken only by Moses? hath He not spoken also by us? The LORD heard. Moses was very meek, above all men on the face of the earth. Guidance: Sibling jealousy disguised as racial criticism, dressed up as a spiritual complaint. God heard every word. The narrator pauses to tell us: Moses is meek — meaning he doesn't fight back. He doesn't have to.

The LORD suddenly to Moses, Aaron, Miriam: come out you three to the tabernacle. The three came. The LORD came down in the pillar of the cloud, stood in the door, called Aaron and Miriam; both came forth. Guidance: God calls a family meeting and chooses the venue Himself. The pillar comes down, the siblings step forward. Sometimes only God's voice can settle a complaint that started at home.

The LORD suddenly to Moses, Aaron, Miriam: come out you three to the tabernacle. The three came. The LORD came down in the pillar of the cloud, stood in the door, called Aaron and Miriam; both came forth. Guidance: God calls a family meeting and chooses the venue Himself. The pillar comes down, the siblings step forward. Sometimes only God's voice can settle a complaint that started at home.

Hear My words: if a prophet, I make Myself known in vision, speak in a dream. Not so My servant Moses — faithful in all My house. With him I speak mouth to mouth, plainly. Why were ye not afraid to speak against him? The LORD's anger kindled; He departed. Guidance: Other prophets get visions and dreams; Moses gets face-time. God defends him publicly. Speaking against God's friend is not a small thing — even his own family learns this the hard way.

The cloud departed from off the tabernacle; behold, Miriam leprous, white as snow. Aaron looked upon her — and behold, she was leprous. Guidance: Miriam alone is struck — perhaps because she led the complaint. Aaron's role as priest spares him, but he watches his sister's skin turn. Sin against God's anointed has a face the next morning.

Aaron to Moses: alas my lord, lay not the sin upon us, wherein we have done foolishly and sinned. Let her not be as one dead. Moses cried to the LORD: heal her now, O God, I beseech Thee. Guidance: The brother Miriam wronged is the first to pray for her. Twelve words to God: heal her now. The greatest gift one sibling gives another is intercession before vindication.

Aaron to Moses: alas my lord, lay not the sin upon us, wherein we have done foolishly and sinned. Let her not be as one dead. Moses cried to the LORD: heal her now, O God, I beseech Thee. Guidance: The brother Miriam wronged is the first to pray for her. Twelve words to God: heal her now. The greatest gift one sibling gives another is intercession before vindication.

The LORD: if her father had but spit in her face, should she not be ashamed seven days? Shut her out seven days; after let her be received. Miriam was shut out; the people journeyed not till she was brought in. Removed to Paran. Guidance: A week of public shame, then full restoration. The whole camp waits — six hundred thousand people pause until one woman is healed. God's discipline is real and so is His patience to gather her back.
